meaning: n.鱼雷,油井爆破筒v.用鱼雷进攻,破坏
examples: The torpedo struck home on the hull of the ship.
But the legacy he leaves behind is so rich, so deep, that no scandal can torpedo it.
Torpedo rays (Torpedo californica) are identifiable by their flat gray bodies and black spots.
She had an idea for radio-controlled torpedo delivery that could not be foiled by the enemy.
The military used Ashaig as part of the Inner Sound of Raasay torpedo testing range.
